title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 90.
content:
There was an Old Person of Spain,
    Who hated all trouble and pain;
    So he sate on a chair,
    With his feet in the air,
    That umbrageous Old Person of Spain.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 90.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
